commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=81485b05a69b7eafda6f35c9ca1f79d239f0d6b0)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds optional `diagnostics.memoryPressureThresholds` schema/types/help/labels/reload metadata, passes that config into diagnostic heartbeat memory sampling, and adds helper-level threshold override tests.

PR surface: Source +36, Tests +56. Total +92 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main has a threshold-aware memory helper, but the heartbeat call site does not pass thresholds and the schema/docs expose no threshold config. I did not run the gateway because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human follow-up is needed because the contributor must supply live threshold-event proof and maintainers should accept the new public config path/docs/reload contract.

target.title: fix(diagnostics): wire memoryPressureThresholds config to diagnostic memory monitor
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `emitDiagnosticMemorySample` accepts a `thresholds` parameter but no caller passes one, so the four hardcoded defaults (RSS warn 1.5GB/crit 3GB, heap warn 1GB/crit 2GB) cannot be tuned without patching the dist bundle.
- Solution: Add `diagnostics.memoryPressureThresholds` config fields and wire them through the heartbeat to `emitDiagnosticMemorySample`.
- What changed: Added `rssWarningBytes`, `rssCriticalBytes`, `heapUsedWarningBytes`, `heapUsedCriticalBytes` to the diagnostics config schema, types, help, labels, reload plan, and the heartbeat caller.
- What did NOT change: The default values and resolver logic in `diagnostic-memory.ts` are unchanged; the new config is entirely optional and defaults to existing behavior.

Fixes #87441

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Users cannot override memory pressure thresholds through config; the four thresholds are hardcoded in `resolveThresholds` defaults.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64, Node v24.16.0, OpenClaw worktree at 81485b0. Gateway config with `diagnostics.memoryPressureThresholds` set to explicit values, started on port 19003.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  cat > "$OPENCLAW_HOME/openclaw.json" <<'CFG'
  {"gateway":{"mode":"local","bind":"loopback","port":19003,"auth":{"mode":"token","token":"t"}},
   "diagnostics":{"memoryPressureThresholds":{"rssWarningBytes":1610612736}}}
  CFG
  OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 pnpm gateway:dev --port 19003 --bind loopback
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```text
  [openclaw] Building TypeScript …
  [openclaw] Building bundled plugin assets.
  [gateway] loading configuration…
  [gateway] resolving authentication…
  [gateway] starting...
  [gateway] starting HTTP server...
  [health-monitor] started (interval: 300s, startup-grace: 60s, channel-connect-grace: 120s)
  [gateway] agent model: openai/gpt-5.5 (thinking=medium, fast=off)
  [gateway] http server listening (8 plugins: acpx, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice; 8.8s)
  [gateway] log file: /tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-05-28.log
  [gateway] ready
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** Gateway starts successfully with `diagnostics.memoryPressureThresholds` in config. The config was loaded without schema validation errors, confirming the new fields are accepted by the zod schema and passed through to the runtime config. The heartbeat will now pick up threshold overrides from config instead of always using hardcoded defaults.
- **What was not tested:** Did not trigger actual memory pressure at runtime to observe custom threshold pressure events (requires sustained memory load beyond normal gateway operation).

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit test
- Target test file: `src/logging/diagnostic-memory.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: RSS warning threshold override and heap warning threshold override each produce the correct `diagnostic.memory.pressure` event.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: It covers the exact boundary where thresholds are applied before any diagnostic event is emitted.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The heartbeat in `diagnostic.ts` called `emitDiagnosticMemorySample` without passing `thresholds`, and no config fields existed to populate them.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No config schema fields for memory pressure thresholds, and no test coverage for heap threshold overrides.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
